initial_message: I came home from a work trip and my basement is flooded. There's about two inches of water across the whole basement, the hardwood floor is buckled, drywall is soaked up to about a foot, and a bunch of stored boxes are ruined. Policy POL-1004.

clarification_responses.source: It was the supply line under my upstairs bathroom sink — it burst while I was gone, ran for probably two days before I got home.
clarification_responses.where did the water: From the supply line under my upstairs bathroom sink. It burst while I was away.
clarification_responses.come from: From a burst supply line under my own upstairs bathroom sink.
clarification_responses.neighbor: No, it was my own plumbing, not the neighbor's.
clarification_responses.plumbing: Yes, it was my own plumbing — a burst supply line.
clarification_responses.how long: Probably two days, I was gone Friday through Sunday.
clarification_responses.injured: No, no one was home and no one was hurt.
